name: Ecstatic dance trance ob3ect: ritual dance that induces altered states through repetitive movement, breath, and drumming until the dancer transcends ordinary consciousness. FSPLIT: the dance splits the practitioner into the dancing body (autonomous, moving to rhythm) and the witnessing awareness (detached, observing the body dance). FFUSE: at the peak of ecstasy, body and awareness reconstitute into a unified transcendent state — the dancer becomes the dance. VINIT: stillness before the first drumbeat. TANCH: the dance circle. AFWD: movement outward — expansion. AREV: movement inward — contraction. ENGAGR: the ecstatic state where the dancer is simultaneously fully embodied (every cell awake) and fully disembodied (consciousness floating above).

phase_0.domain_name: Ecstatic dance trance ob3ect: ritual dance that induces altered states through repetitive movement, breath, and drumming until the dancer transcends ordinary consciousness. FSPLIT: the dance splits the practitioner into the dancing body (autonomous, moving to rhythm) and the witnessing awareness (detached, observing the body dance). FFUSE: at the peak of ecstasy, body and awareness reconstitute into a unified transcendent state — the dancer becomes the dance. VINIT: stillness before the first drumbeat. TANCH: the dance circle. AFWD: movement outward — expansion. AREV: movement inward — contraction. ENGAGR: the ecstatic state where the dancer is simultaneously fully embodied (every cell awake) and fully disembodied (consciousness floating above).

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any movement or awareness arises

TANCH.justification: closed boundary that contains the entire ritual space and all transformations

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: directed transformation toward reaching beyond ordinary limits

AREV.justification: reverse/descent: returning toward center, grounding, introversion

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential chaining of movement phases (e.g., expansion then contraction then expansion)
CLINK.justification: composition: linking multiple morphisms in sequence to build trance

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the dancer's self-recognition as both mover and observer
IMSCRIB.justification: identity: the core 'I' that remains self-aware through the dance

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the dance splits the practitioner into dancing body and witnessing awareness
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication: one conscious entity branches into autonomous body and detached observer

FFUSE.chosen_element: at peak ecstasy, body and awareness reconstitute into unified transcendent state
FFUSE.justification: multiplication: the two branches fuse back into the original unified dancer, now transcendent

EVALT.chosen_element: successful trance induction — dancer reaches ecstatic unity
EVALT.justification: true/affirmative: the ritual achieves its goal

EVALF.chosen_element: failure to enter trance — dancer remains stuck in ordinary dual awareness
EVALF.justification: false/negative: the split persists without fusion

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the ecstatic state where dancer is simultaneously fully embodied and fully disembodied
ENGAGR.justification: both: paradox of being wholly present and transcendent at once

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent memory of the ecstatic experience etched into the dancer's psyche
IFIX.justification: irreversible fixation: the transformation becomes a lasting record

phase_2.split_element: the dance splits the practitioner into dancing body and witnessing awareness
phase_2.fuse_element: at peak ecstasy, body and awareness reconstitute into unified transcendent state

phase_3.void_description: 00 — absolute stillness before the first drumbeat, no movement, no awareness
phase_3.true_description: 01 — ecstatic unity achieved, dancer is the dance
phase_3.false_description: 10 — split persists, no fusion, ordinary consciousness
phase_3.both_description: 11 — dancer simultaneously fully embodied and fully disembodied

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the dancer acknowledges their own presence and intention to dance
Step 2 AREV:   the dancer takes a deep centering breath, descending into the body
Step 3 FSPLIT: the dancer begins repetitive movement; awareness detaches from the body
Step 4 AFWD:   the body expands movement outward; awareness rises upward
Step 5 FFUSE:  at the peak, body and awareness merge into one transcendent state
Step 6 CLINK:  the dancer links this peak experience with the ongoing rhythm and breath
Step 7 IFIX:   the dancer records the experience in memory, perhaps with a gesture or cry
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the dancer returns to self-recognition, now transformed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the shaman or drum rhythm that translates intention into movement
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: rhythmic drumming and breath synchronize body and awareness
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the dancer's body and psyche store the trance state
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the drum beat and breath cycle determine timing of movements
phase_5.alfs_store: the ancestral tradition of ecstatic dance (e.g., Sufi whirling, San healing dance)

phase_6.cycle_cost: expended physical energy and focused attention
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: ordinary dual consciousness (body separate from awareness)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: unified transcendent consciousness (body and awareness one)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle conserves the dancer's essential self; energy is transformed, not dissipated
